key_departures.impact: Traded to Dallas for a 2027 4th-round pick. Gary delivered zero sacks or TFLs in the final 9 games of 2025 and was a cap casualty as much as a trade asset. Green Bay saved ~$11M. Still, his departure leaves a thinned EDGE room with Parsons still rehabbing a torn ACL.
Agreed to a 1-year, $10M deal with the Jets. Was a key rotation pass rusher for Green Bay. His departure further depletes an already thin EDGE room alongside the Gary trade.

Signed a 3-year, $40.5M deal ($28M guaranteed) with the Raiders. Four-year starter and team tackle leader every season. The Packers preemptively traded for Zaire Franklin but Walker's departure is still a significant loss at LB.

Agreed to a 4-year, $70M deal with the Patriots. Led the Packers in receiving in 2025 (55 catches, 724 yards, 6 TDs). A real loss as WR1, though Matthew Golden and Christian Watson are in place as replacements.

Signed a 3-year, $67.5M deal ($45M guaranteed) with the Dolphins. Was Jordan Love's backup and performed exceptionally in limited action. Leaves Green Bay without a proven QB2 but nets the Packers a projected 3rd-round compensatory pick in 2027.

Signed a 1-year, $10M deal with Carolina. Was GB's starting LT for 3 straight seasons (48 starts). Departure creates a left tackle opening, though first-round pick Jordan Morgan is now in line to take over the job.

Released by the Packers, saving $19.5M in cap space. Jenkins suffered a season-ending ankle injury in 2025 and was supplanted at center by Sean Rhyan. A 2x Pro Bowler, but the release was primarily cap-driven and need was already backstopped by Rhyan's re-signing.

Released as a post-June 1 designation, freeing $8.8M in cap space this summer. Hobbs was signed to a 4-year, $48M deal last offseason but played only 358 snaps over 11 games due to injuries and poor performance. His release escalates an existing CB need.

Traded to Indy as part of the Zaire Franklin acquisition. Wooden started 16 games in 2025 but struggled with run defense consistency. His departure creates an IDL depth hole that necessitated the Hargrave signing.
UFA recovering from torn Achilles, will miss the 2026 season. Combined with Tucker Kraft's torn ACL recovery, the Packers TE room entered free agency severely depleted.

signings.analysis: Acquired via trade (Colby Wooden to Indy), no guaranteed money remaining on his 3-year, $31.26M extension. Franklin led the NFL in tackles in 2024 (173) and earned second-team All-Pro honors, but regressed in 2025. His coverage limitations are a known concern. At ~$7M/year with no guarantees, this is a cost-controlled starter rather than a long-term cornerstone. LB goes from a rising need to a partially addressed one. The team still needs to develop Edgerrin Cooper and likely wants draft capital here.

3-year, $33M re-signing ($11M signing bonus, max value $39M) locks Rhyan in as the starting center after he excelled in that role in the back half of 2025 following Jenkins' ankle injury. The Packers have committed to Rhyan as their starting center moving forward. Combined with Aaron Banks at guard and Jordan Morgan/Anthony Belton at the other guard, the IOL picture is largely set. Structurally, $11M guaranteed on a 3-year deal is modest — functionally closer to a 1-year starter deal with team options — but the Packers view Rhyan as their long-term center. IOL need is fully addressed at C; guard depth still worth monitoring.

2-year, $23M deal ($13M guaranteed in year 1; year 2 is effectively a team option). Hargrave is a two-time Pro Bowler with 49 career sacks who reunites with DC Jonathan Gannon, under whom he posted 18.5 sacks across 2021-22 in Philadelphia. Addresses the gaping hole left by the Kenny Clark trade and Colby Wooden departure. However, Hargrave is 33 and coming off a down 2025 (3.5 sacks). This is a bridge/veteran starter, not a long-term IDL cornerstone. The Packers still need IDL depth and likely want a younger piece via the draft. Need drops from critical to moderate.

2-year, $10M deal ($3M guaranteed) with the Commanders' 2021 3rd-round pick. St-Juste played only 35% of defensive snaps for the Chargers in 2025 (2 starts) but posted a strong 68.3 passer rating allowed in coverage. At $5M AAV this is firmly a depth/competition signing — well below starter-tier CB money. With Nate Hobbs released and the CB room thin (Nixon, Valentine, St-Juste), CB remains a genuine need heading into the draft. St-Juste adds competition and a Gannon-system fit but doesn't resolve the boundary CB problem.

1-year deal ($2.5M base, up to $4.5M with incentives). Signed primarily as a return specialist — Moore ranked 6th in the NFL in kickoff return average (27.5 yds) and 7th in punt return average (11.6 yds) with the 49ers in 2025. A direct answer to the Packers finishing dead last in punt return average (5.6 yds) in 2025. Limited offensive role expected (~100 snaps). Does not address the WR need left by Romeo Doubs — this is a special teams acquisition.

1-year, $2.5M re-signing. Cox had 4 sacks and 7 QB hits in 2024 but missed 13 games in 2025 with a groin injury. At best, he's the 3rd-4th edge rusher behind Parsons (rehabbing torn ACL) and Karl Brooks. One-year deal signals a prove-it contract, not a solution. EDGE remains a critical need — Parsons may miss the start of the season, Gary and Enagbare are gone, and Cox is injury-prone.

1-year deal worth up to $3.75M (including $1M in playtime incentives). Kinnard was a restricted free agent who played 285 offensive snaps as a swing tackle/6th lineman in 2025. Provides crucial depth insurance while Zach Tom recovers from torn patella tendon surgery. Does not address the LT starter void left by Rasheed Walker — Jordan Morgan is expected to take over that role. Depth signing only.

1-year, $2M re-signing of the restricted free agent who emerged as TE3 last season after Tucker Kraft tore his ACL and John FitzPatrick tore his Achilles. Whyle provides necessary roster depth but is not a starter-caliber TE. With Kraft's return timeline uncertain, Musgrave as TE2, and FitzPatrick out for the year, TE remains a need that should be addressed in the draft.

1-year minimum deal to retain the 2022 7th-round pick who returned to Green Bay in December 2025 after the Bears waived him. Provides nose tackle depth after Colby Wooden was traded away. At minimum salary, this is a camp/roster body, not a true IDL need address.

Re-signed on a minimum deal primarily for special teams. Made 3 tackles in 8 games before going on IR in 2025. LB depth/ST contributor only — does not affect the Walker-sized hole at the starter position.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Sean Rhyan re-signed on a 3-year, $33M deal ($11M signing bonus) as the starting center. Combined with Aaron Banks at guard, Jordan Morgan's OL versatility, and Anthony Belton, the interior line is set. Jenkins' release is net-neutral given Rhyan directly replaces him. IOL is no longer a meaningful draft need — at most a Day 3 developmental pick.

Quay Walker's departure (3yr/$40.5M to Raiders) was offset by the Zaire Franklin trade from Indianapolis (Colby Wooden sent back). Franklin is a proven starter (2024 All-Pro, led NFL in tackles) and reunites with DC Gannon. However, Franklin has no guaranteed money remaining, had a down 2025, and struggles in coverage. This is a bridge solution alongside Edgerrin Cooper. LB drops from critical to moderate — a mid-round draft pick to develop depth/succession remains prudent.

Javon Hargrave (2yr/$23M, $13M gtd) steps in as the starting DT alongside Devonte Wyatt, reuniting with DC Jonathan Gannon under whom he produced 18.5 sacks across two Eagles seasons. This meaningfully addresses the IDL starter hole created by the Kenny Clark trade and Colby Wooden departure. However, Hargrave is 33, on a bridge deal, and Jonathan Ford (minimum) is the only other IDL addition. IDL drops from tier 1 critical to tier 3 moderate. A young IDL piece in the draft (rounds 2-4) remains valuable for succession planning.

This is now the team's most critical need. Rashan Gary was traded to Dallas, Kingsley Enagbare departed to the Jets (1yr/$10M), and Micah Parsons is recovering from a torn ACL and may miss the start of the season. The re-signing of Brenton Cox Jr. (1yr/$2.5M) is a depth move only — Cox missed 13 games in 2025. Karl Brooks is the only other viable edge option. The Packers go from thin to potentially starting the season with no healthy starting-caliber EDGE rusher. Should be the primary draft target in April. Escalated to tier 1.

The CB room is slightly worse post-FA despite the St-Juste signing. Nate Hobbs was cut (post-June 1 designation, saving $8.8M), removing a $48M bust from the books. Benjamin St-Juste (2yr/$10M, $3M gtd) is a depth/competition signing, not a starter-level investment. The Packers return Keisean Nixon and Carrington Valentine as their primary starters, but both are inconsistent boundary corners. St-Juste doesn't change the need tier meaningfully. CB remains a tier 2 need heading into the draft.

Romeo Doubs departed to New England on a 4-year, $70M deal, removing the team's 2025 leading receiver (55 rec, 724 yds, 6 TDs). Matthew Golden and Christian Watson are the primary returning WRs, but neither is proven as a true WR1. Skyy Moore (1yr/$2.5M) is purely a returner with minimal offensive upside. Green Bay has no proven top-end WR behind Watson/Golden. Need escalates from tier 3 to tier 2 — a WR2 type in rounds 2-3 would provide needed depth and competition.

Rasheed Walker departed on a 1-year, $10M deal with Carolina, leaving Green Bay without its starting left tackle of the past three seasons. Jordan Morgan (2024 1st-round pick) is expected to slide into the LT role, and Zach Tom remains at RT (though recovering from torn patella surgery). Darian Kinnard re-signed as a swing tackle. Jordan Morgan is unproven as a full-time LT starter — his 2025 was largely as a backup. OT escalates to tier 2. A developmental OT in the draft or one more veteran acquisition before the season is advisable.

Tucker Kraft (torn ACL) has an uncertain return timeline, John FitzPatrick (torn Achilles) will miss the entire 2026 season, and Luke Musgrave failed to seize the TE1 role when given the opportunity. Josh Whyle (1yr/$2M) is a depth piece only. The Packers go into the draft with one functioning, proven TE on the roster (Musgrave as the de facto starter) and serious depth concerns. TE should be a top-5 draft priority. Escalated to tier 1.

Malik Willis' departure (3yr/$67.5M to Dolphins) leaves Green Bay without a proven backup behind Jordan Love. No QB2 has been added yet. While Jordan Love's health is the primary variable and the team has ~$20M remaining cap space, operating through a full 2026 season without a capable backup is a genuine roster construction concern. Need tier rises from 4 (non-issue) to 3 (moderate). A veteran bridge QB via free agency or late-round draft pick is expected.

key_additions.headline: 2024 All-Pro tackler (173 tackles, league leader) replaces Quay Walker via player-for-player trade with Colts
Two-time Pro Bowl DT reunites with DC Jonathan Gannon to anchor the defensive interior vacated by the Kenny Clark trade
Breakout center locked up long-term after taking over the position mid-2025 following Elgton Jenkins' ankle injury
6-foot-3 boundary corner adds size and competition to a CB room depleted by Nate Hobbs' release
Top-10 kickoff and punt returner in 2025 surgically repairs the team's dead-last return game

key_losses.headline: Team's leading receiver in 2025 (55 rec, 724 yds, 6 TDs) departs on a massive deal, leaving WR depth concerns
Elite backup who posted 134.6 passer rating departs as a full-time starter, netting Packers projected 3rd-round comp pick
Four-year team tackle leader departs; only Packer to lead the team in tackles in each of his first 4 seasons
Three-year starting LT (48 starts) heads to Carolina on a prove-it deal, opening the door for Jordan Morgan at left tackle
Former 1st-round pick moved for cap relief, further thinning an EDGE room already without a healthy Micah Parsons
2x Pro Bowl lineman released for $19.5M in cap savings after ankle injury; Rhyan re-signing made him expendable
$48M free agent bust from 2025 released after one injury-shortened, underwhelming season in Green Bay
